audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Judges / Shoftim remaining-nations test list and first deliverer narratives, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for remaining nations, intermarriage/cultic assimilation, Othniel, Cushan-rishathaim, Ehud's left-handed assassination of Eglon, escape and Moab defeat, and Shamgar's oxgoad notice.

verses.restored: These are nations YHWH left to test Israel by them, all who had not known all wars of Canaan,
only so generations of children of Israel would know, to teach them war—only those who had not formerly known them:
five rulers of Philistines, all Canaanites, Sidonians, and Hivites dwelling at Mount Lebanon from Mount Baal-hermon as far as Lebo-hamath.
They were for testing Israel by them, to know whether they would listen to commands of YHWH that he commanded their fathers by hand of Moses.
Children of Israel dwelt in midst of Canaanite, Hittite, Amorite, Perizzite, Hivite, and Jebusite.
They took their daughters for themselves as wives, and their own daughters they gave to their sons, and they served their gods.
Children of Israel did evil in eyes of YHWH; they forgot YHWH their Divine and served the Baals and Asherahs.
Anger of YHWH burned against Israel, and he sold them into hand of Cushan-rishathaim king of Aram-naharaim; children of Israel served Cushan-rishathaim eight years.
Children of Israel cried out to YHWH, and YHWH raised a savior/deliverer for children of Israel, and he saved them: Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.
Spirit of YHWH was upon him, and he judged Israel. He went out to war, and YHWH gave Cushan-rishathaim king of Aram into his hand; his hand prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim.
Land was quiet forty years. Then Othniel son of Kenaz died.
Children of Israel again did evil in eyes of YHWH, and YHWH strengthened Eglon king of Moab against Israel because they did evil in eyes of YHWH.
He gathered to himself sons of Ammon and Amalek; he went and struck Israel, and they possessed city of palms.
Children of Israel served Eglon king of Moab eighteen years.

Children of Israel cried out to YHWH, and YHWH raised for them a savior/deliverer: Ehud son of Gera, Benjamite, a man bound/restricted in his right hand. Children of Israel sent tribute by his hand to Eglon king of Moab.
Ehud made for himself a sword with two edges, a cubit its length, and he strapped it under his garment on his right thigh.
He presented tribute to Eglon king of Moab; Eglon was a very fat man.
It was, when he finished presenting tribute, that he sent away people carrying tribute.
But he himself turned back from carved stones/idols near Gilgal and said, A secret word I have for you, O king. He said, Silence. All those standing by him went out from him.
Ehud came to him while he was sitting alone in cool upper chamber. Ehud said, A word of Divine I have for you. He rose from his seat.
Ehud stretched out his left hand, took sword from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly.
Even handle went in after blade, and fat closed over blade, for he did not draw sword from his belly; and refuse came out.
Ehud went out by porch, shut doors of upper chamber behind him, and locked them.
He went out, and servants came; they saw, and look, doors of upper chamber were locked. They said, Surely he is covering his feet in cool room.
They waited until ashamed, and look, he was not opening doors of upper chamber. They took key and opened, and look, their lord had fallen to ground, dead.
Ehud escaped while they delayed; he passed carved stones/idols and escaped to Seirah.
It was, when he came, that he blew shofar in hill country of Ephraim, and children of Israel went down with him from hill country, and he before them.
He said to them, Pursue after me, for YHWH has given your enemies, Moab, into your hand. They went down after him and captured fords of Jordan against Moab and did not allow a man to cross.
They struck Moab at that time about ten thousand men, every strong and every man of valor; no man escaped.
Moab was subdued that day under hand of Israel, and land was quiet eighty years.
After him was Shamgar son of Anath; he struck Philistine, six hundred men, with oxgoad; he also saved Israel.

translation_notes: Verses 1-2 explain remaining nations as a test and a means of teaching war. This is descriptive theology within Judges and must not become a modern argument that young people need war to become faithful.
Intermarriage language is tied to cultic assimilation in the narrator's covenant frame and must not be used for racism, xenophobia, or anti-intermarriage ideology.
Othniel's story is brief and paradigmatic: cry, raised deliverer, Spirit, victory, quiet, death.
Ehud is described as 'restricted/bound in his right hand' or left-handed; the phrase should not be treated as disability shame. His difference becomes tactically significant in the narrative.
Eglon's fatness is part of the assassination scene's grotesque satire. Modern reception must reject body shaming and anti-fat mockery.

The Ehud narrative uses deception, assassination, toilet humor, and slaughter of fleeing Moabites. It may be preserved as ancient resistance narrative but not preached as a model for political murder.
Shamgar's oxgoad notice is terse, violent, and historically obscure; 'son of Anath' may carry complex cultural or divine-war associations requiring audit.
safety_note: Do not use Judges 3 to glorify assassination, mock fat bodies, shame disability, promote ethnic purity, or argue that war is spiritually necessary. Center the cry for deliverance, the danger of oppression, and careful refusal to turn survival violence into a general moral template.
